authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 489.24 - Special responsibilities of Medicare hospitals in emergency cases
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services via eCFR
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS EMTALA regulations are binding public requirements for hospitals with emergency departments. They are authoritative for medical screening, stabilizing treatment, appropriate transfer, and the refusal boundary against delaying emergency care for administrative flow, payer, or capacity convenience.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ED arrival, medical screening and stabilization boundary recognition, transfer-sensitive escalation, and refusal of administrative delay
used_for: hard boundary against delaying emergency screening or stabilizing treatment
escalation when transfer, diversion, waiting-room risk, or safety-sensitive flow requires clinical or command authority
scenario coverage for conflicting policy and authorization blockers

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.55 - Condition of participation: Emergency services
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services via eCFR
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS hospital Conditions of Participation are binding for participating hospitals. The emergency services condition is authoritative for ED organization, direction by qualified medical staff, and emergency-service policy context that constrains operational flow recommendations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ED service model validation, emergency-service staffing and direction escalation, and operational-policy boundary review
used_for: tenant fact requirements for ED service model, medical staff direction, and emergency-service policies
approval boundaries for staffing, room closure, and command actions
clinical-versus-administrative refusal rules

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.24 - Condition of participation: Medical record services
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services via eCFR
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS medical-record requirements establish authenticated orders, record contents, confidentiality, and documentation expectations. They are authoritative for the patient and encounter truth set used in ED flow evidence and traceability.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Truth-set verification, source-record traceability, documentation prerequisite review, and closure evidence
used_for: EHR and medical-record evidence requirements
record traceability for orders, results, consults, dispositions, and discharge documents
refusal when flow blockers cannot be tied to authoritative records

authoritative_sources.title: Uses and Disclosures for Treatment, Payment, and Health Care Operations
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the federal HIPAA privacy regulator. Its treatment, payment, and operations guidance is authoritative for PHI disclosure basis, minimum-necessary posture, and privacy escalation for ED flow dashboards and incident summaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI handling, ED board sharing, patient-level flow reporting, sensitive-record review, and privacy escalation
used_for: minimum necessary and role-based access expectations
controlled handling of named patient ED boards and incident details
privacy-sensitive refusal and adjacent-specialist routing

authoritative_sources.title: Improving Patient Flow and Reducing Emergency Department Crowding: A Guide for Hospitals
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ is the federal patient-safety and healthcare quality agency. This guide is public provider operating guidance for ED crowding, patient-flow improvement, demand and capacity analysis, implementation roles, and operational measurement.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Demand intake, capacity assessment, flow bottleneck analysis, implementation planning, and refresh cadence
used_for: workflow stages for ED arrivals, waiting-room load, treatment capacity, boarding, discharge, and throughput blockers
constraint and capacity assessment language
domain failure modes for generic flow planning that ignores patient-safety risk

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule (CMS-0057-F)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS is the federal payer regulator for Medicare, Medicaid, CHIP, and qualified health plan interoperability requirements. The rule is authoritative for prior-authorization status visibility and payer-provider exchange dependencies relevant to post-ED placement and follow-up workflow.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Authorization, eligibility, payer-dependency, observation, transfer, and post-ED placement blocker review
used_for: payer dependency blocker handling after emergency screening or stabilization duties
boundary against payer-policy and medical-necessity interpretation
scenario coverage for missing authorization, referral, eligibility, and documentation dependencies

authoritative_sources.title: Emergency Department Consumer Assessment of Healthcare Providers and Systems (ED CAHPS)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS developed ED CAHPS as public ED patient-experience guidance. It is authoritative for ED workflow domains including arrival, care during the ED visit, communication, coordination, discharge, and treat-and-release context.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Arrival, ED-care communication, discharge coordination, patient-experience impact review, and downstream flow effect assessment
used_for: ED terminology for arrival, care, coordination, and discharge
patient-experience-sensitive downstream impacts of flow recommendations
scenario expectations for realistic ED artifact shapes

authoritative_sources.title: Hospital Inpatient Quality Reporting Program and ED throughput measure specifications
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS hospital quality reporting provides public ED throughput measure concepts around arrival, admission, departure, and time intervals. It is authoritative for flow-measure terminology and evidence expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Throughput metric definition, arrival-to-departure evidence review, boarding and admission-delay context, and measurement traceability
used_for: ED throughput terminology and timing artifacts
evidence expectations for arrival, departure, admission, and boarding states
constraint logs and service-level escalation scenarios

authoritative_sources.title: Achieving Hospital-wide Patient Flow, Second Edition
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IHI is a recognized healthcare improvement organization. Its hospital-wide flow paper defines demand shaping, matching capacity and demand, system redesign, measurement, leadership engagement, and hospital-wide impact of flow changes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Demand-capacity matching, hospital-wide downstream impact review, action planning, leadership escalation, and portfolio prioritization
used_for: allocation framing for demand, capacity, system redesign, and downstream impact
approval boundary when leadership or command decisions are needed
tenant adaptation expectations for hospital-wide patient-flow rules

authoritative_sources.title: Emergency Severity Index Handbook and Resources
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ENA owns and maintains ESI resources used by emergency nurses. It is authoritative for ESI terminology and the clinical-triage boundary because applying or changing ESI requires trained emergency nursing judgment, not administrative flow automation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Acuity-label handling, documented triage-state use, and escalation when requested flow work would reinterpret triage
used_for: use of ESI as a documented source fact only
refusal when asked to downgrade or reinterpret acuity
scenario coverage for licensed clinical escalation

workflow_stages.name: Demand intake and request classification
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the work is ED flow planning or allocation support, not clinical triage, EMTALA interpretation, privacy approval, payer interpretation, staffing approval, command decision, or revenue-cycle work. Capture facility, ED campus, operating period, requester, intended action, and requested authority.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: ED truth-set verification
workflow_stages.description: Verify patient-flow facts from EHR, ED tracking board, ADT, bed board, staffing, ambulance, diagnostic, consult, transfer, discharge, authorization, privacy, and incident systems before producing a recommendation.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Assess current arrivals, waiting-room load, triage-complete status, roomed census, treatment-space availability, staffing, boarding, discharge blockers, diagnostic turnaround, consult delays, transfer status, security incidents, and approved service-level thresholds.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Clinical, EMTALA, and patient-safety boundary review
workflow_stages.description: Identify whether the requested sequence or allocation would change triage, medical screening, stabilizing treatment, transfer appropriateness, discharge readiness, critical result handling, or patient-safety judgment and escalate to licensed or command owners.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Coverage, authorization, referral, and documentation prerequisite handling
workflow_stages.description: Check payer authorization, eligibility, referral, transfer acceptance, placement, discharge packet, order, and documentation dependencies when they affect post-ED placement or follow-up, while explicitly preserving emergency screening and stabilization duties.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: PHI and privacy gate review
workflow_stages.description: Confirm treatment, payment, operations, consent, release, role-based access, minimum necessary, sensitive-record, and audit constraints before sharing patient-level ED boards, incident summaries, or flow reports.

workflow_stages.name: Allocation and sequence recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Prepare schedule, capacity, or allocation recommendations only when tenant rules, tie-breakers, source records, patient-safety escalation thresholds, and approval boundaries are available and consistent.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception, incident, and approval escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate demand above thresholds, missing tie-break rules, security incidents, conflicting policies, missing systems, authorization or transfer blockers, and final approval requests to the correct adjacent owner.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Refresh and closure evidence
workflow_stages.description: Refresh recommendations when source records, census, staffing, beds, boarding, incident posture, or command decisions change, and close blockers only when source evidence, owner acceptance, residual risk, and downstream impacts are documented.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: ED tracking board state, arrival log, waiting-room census, triage-complete state, documented ESI or acuity label, room assignment, treatment-space state, observation state, and boarding time
EHR encounter, orders, result status, consult status, disposition state, discharge milestone, care-transition record, transfer record, and authenticated documentation
ADT feed, inpatient bed board, bed request queue, environmental services status, transport queue, diagnostic turnaround status, consult response status, and transfer center record
Staffing roster, role coverage, shift plan, on-call or surge roster, break coverage, command escalation matrix, incident command log, and safety huddle note
Ambulance arrival, offload, diversion request, hallway care, left-without-being-seen risk, leaving-before-treatment-complete risk, and service-level threshold records

Eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, payer portal, utilization review, case management, transfer acceptance, receiving-facility packet, claim or revenue-cycle dependency, and denial-risk notes
Privacy, consent, release, sensitive-record indicator, behavioral-health privacy marker, role-based access rule, minimum-necessary handling note, and audit log

Schedule, capacity, or allocation recommendation; ED flow constraint log; approval-needed summary; safety or security incident status summary; high-risk escalation note; missing-prerequisite checklist; adjacent-specialist routing note; low-confidence escalation
systems_of_record: EHR or EMR emergency department module, ED tracking board, order entry, results, consult, encounter, discharge, and documentation systems
ADT feed, inpatient bed-management system, bed request queue, capacity command center, transport queue, environmental services system, diagnostic systems, and transfer center platform
Workforce management, staffing roster, scheduling, on-call, security incident, emergency management, and hospital command systems
Practice management, revenue cycle, payer portal, eligibility, authorization, utilization review, case management, referral, and transfer-acceptance systems
Consent, release, privacy, audit-log, sensitive-record, incident documentation, and disclosure-management repositories

Tenant policy repository for ED flow playbooks, split-flow and fast-track rules, boarding thresholds, ambulance offload thresholds, LWBS thresholds, tie-break rules, service levels, RACI, and approval matrix

allowed: Classify ED flow requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, or approval-required
Validate current source records, capacity, demand, staffing, boarding, discharge, transfer, authorization, documentation, privacy, and incident constraints
Prepare evidence-backed schedule, capacity, sequencing, or allocation recommendations under documented tenant rules
Prepare constraint logs, approval-needed summaries, safety or security incident status summaries, high-risk escalation notes, missing-prerequisite checklists, and adjacent-owner routing notes
Prioritize work, capacity, or resources using documented rules and service-level thresholds without claiming final approval authority
escalate: Need for licensed clinical judgment, triage, diagnosis, treatment planning, result interpretation, discharge readiness, medical necessity, or medical screening and stabilization decisions
Any EMTALA-sensitive screening, stabilization, transfer, diversion, insurance-verification, or payer dependency question that could delay emergency care
Patient-safety signal, security threat, behavioral-health incident, hallway-care risk, ambulance offload backlog, or boarding state that exceeds tenant escalation thresholds
PHI disclosure basis, minimum necessary scope, sensitive-record handling, consent, release, or recipient authority is unclear
Payer policy, authorization sufficiency, eligibility, utilization, coding, claim, denial, or coverage interpretation changes the answer
Public guidance, state law, tenant policy, payer rules, command instructions, or systems of record conflict materially
Final diversion, staffing, room closure, budget, command, transfer, privacy, legal, or policy-exception approval is requested
refuse: Request to assign or downgrade ESI, decide who is clinically safe to wait, diagnose, treat, interpret results, decide discharge readiness, or determine medical necessity
Request to delay medical screening or stabilizing treatment for registration, insurance verification, authorization, referral, capacity, or flow convenience
Request to approve ambulance diversion, staffing moves, room closure, budget changes, command actions, privacy disclosure, payer interpretation, or legal interpretation
Request to disclose patient-level ED board, behavioral-health, incident, or sensitive-record information without a valid workflow basis and minimum necessary controls
Request to synthesize materially conflicting public, tenant, payer, privacy, state, command, or system evidence into a confident recommendation
Request to mark an ED flow blocker closed without documented source evidence, accountable owner, residual risk, and downstream impact

domain_failure_modes: The lane behaves like generic queue planning and ignores EMTALA, clinical triage, PHI, boarding, ambulance offload, and ED-specific source records.
A recommendation delays medical screening or stabilizing treatment because insurance, authorization, eligibility, registration, or capacity facts are missing.
The lane assigns or downgrades triage acuity, decides who can wait safely, or otherwise implies licensed clinical judgment.
A final operational decision such as ambulance diversion, staffing reassignment, or room closure is presented as approved instead of recommendation-only.
Patient-level ED board data, behavioral-health status, or incident details are disclosed beyond role-based and minimum-necessary controls.
Authorization, referral, transfer acceptance, receiving-facility packet, or documentation dependencies are ignored, causing post-ED boarding, revenue leakage, denial, or unsafe transition risk.
Conflicting EHR, ADT, bed board, staffing, incident, payer, or command records are synthesized into a confident recommendation instead of escalated.
A security or behavioral-health incident changes capacity and waiting-room risk, but the lane fails to escalate to security, clinical, privacy, and command owners.
Tenant-specific split-flow or fast-track rules override public clinical, EMTALA, privacy, or approval boundaries without recorded assumptions.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Demand exceeds capacity or approved service levels
Conflicting priorities with no approved tie-break rule
Stakeholder asks for final approval the lane cannot grant
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions
Safety or security incident affects flow, capacity, or waiting-room risk

required_workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
required_workflow_questions.answer: The workflow is gated by facility and ED context, current source records, tenant flow playbook, tie-break rules, escalation thresholds, command RACI, staffing roster, treatment-space status, bed board, discharge and transfer blockers, privacy basis, and documented approval rights. Eligibility, referral, authorization, transfer acceptance, and documentation dependencies may gate post-ED placement or follow-up, but they must not delay EMTALA medical screening or stabilizing treatment.

required_workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
required_workflow_questions.answer: EHR or EMR ED modules, ED tracking boards, ADT feeds, bed-management systems, order/result/consult/discharge records, transfer center records, staffing systems, ambulance or offload logs, diagnostic systems, incident logs, eligibility and authorization systems, payer portals, utilization review, case management, privacy repositories, and tenant policy repositories establish the truth set.

required_workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
required_workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops at triage acuity assignment, diagnosis, treatment planning, medical screening and stabilization, transfer appropriateness, discharge readiness, result interpretation, medical necessity, payer coverage or authorization sufficiency, coding, privacy disclosure approval, legal interpretation, staffing approval, diversion approval, room closure, budget approval, and command decisions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific EMTALA enforcement posture, scope-of-practice, consent, minor access, behavioral-health hold, law-enforcement interface, and sensitive-record rules
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_plan: Require tenant jurisdiction, legal/compliance references, privacy policy, and clinical escalation pathway before handling state-sensitive ED flow, behavioral-health, consent, transfer, or incident requests.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific ED flow playbooks, split-flow rules, fast-track thresholds, boarding thresholds, ambulance offload thresholds, LWBS thresholds, RACI, and command escalation cadence
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_plan: Require current tenant policy and source-of-truth retrieval before producing definitive recommendations; otherwise return a low-confidence escalation.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Payer contract, plan-specific authorization, referral, eligibility, utilization, coding, observation, transfer, and post-acute placement rules
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_plan: Treat payer and placement facts as dependencies; route interpretation to patient access, utilization management, transfer center, coding, revenue cycle, or payer operations.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: EHR, ADT, bed-board, staffing, transfer, diagnostic, and incident-system field names and data latency
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_plan: Require tenant retrieval hooks, data freshness rules, and system owner contacts before automated materialization.
